title: Staff Software Engineer - Applied AI
company_stage: Series C/D (backed by Accel, Y Combinator, Sequoia, BOND, Franklin Templeton)
description:
Ironclad is the leading AI contracting platform that
transforms agreements into assets. Contracts move faster,
insights surface instantly, and agents push work forward,
all with you in control. Whether you’re buying or selling,
Ironclad unifies the entire process on one intelligent
platform, providing leaders with the visibility they need to
stay one step ahead. That’s why the world’s most
transformative organizations, from Rivian to the World
Health Organization and the Associated Press, trust Ironclad
to accelerate their business.

We’re consistently recognized as a leader in the industry: a
Leader in the Forrester Wave and Gartner Magic Quadrant for
Contract Lifecycle Management, a Fortune Great Place to
Work, and one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Workplaces.
Ironclad has also been named to Forbes’ AI 50 and Business
Insider’s list of Companies to Bet Your Career On. We’re
backed by leading investors including Accel, Y Combinator,
Sequoia, BOND, and Franklin Templeton. For more information,
visit www.ironcladapp.com or follow us on LinkedIn.

AI Engineering at Ironclad

Ironclad is accelerating its AI initiatives to transform how
contracts are built, managed, and executed across an
organization. We're looking for a Generative AI Application
Developer to rapidly build and deploy high-impact legal AI
product features.

In this role, you are the expert AI feature developer who
leverages our existing AI platform. You establish a Prompt
Strategy, utilizing established Retrieval-Augmented
Generation (RAG) services and Large Language Model (LLM)
APIs to develop intelligent product capabilities, like
summarization or redlining.

You will partner closely with product managers and designers
to translate user needs into production-ready LLM
application logic. Your success will be measured by your
ability to design the optimal context input and craft
effective prompts to maximize the accuracy, relevance,
latency and reliability of the final user-facing feature.

What you'll be doing:

- Full Stack Development: Lead the design, development, and
  deployment of features from end-to-end using TypeScript,
  Node.js, React, and PostgreSQL, ensuring high performance
  and availability.
- Build Production AI Features: Be a primary contributor to
  the next generation of AI-powered legal features,
  including specialized summarization, drafting, editing,
  and document comparison functionalities utilizing LLMs.
- Architect Highly Scalable AI Products: Build and maintain
  reliable, highly scalable software and services designed
  for a high-traffic SaaS environment.
- Foster Cross-Functional Collaboration: Collaborate closely
  with Product Managers, UX Designers, and QE counterparts
  to rapidly prototype, iterate on designs, and make
  frequent, agile pivots based on user feedback.
- Advocate for the Customer: Maintain a high degree of
  customer empathy, ensuring technical decisions prioritize
  user experience and directly address customer pain points,
  creating measurable product impact.
- Deliver and Optimize AI Infrastructure: Work with platform
  teams to consume and manage internal LLM APIs, ensuring
  resilient connections and efficient token usage to meet
  strict latency targets.

Required Skills:

- Full-Stack Expertise: Deep professional experience with the
  core tech stack: TypeScript, Node.js, React, and
  PostgreSQL.
- High-Scale SaaS Experience: Proven track record of building,
  deploying, and maintaining reliable, highly scalable
  software for SaaS applications.
- LLM Consumption and Integration: Extensive experience
  consuming LLMs via APIs (e.g., OpenAI, Gemini, or
  proprietary models) and integrating them successfully into
  production-facing features.
- AI Feature Implementation: Practical experience building and
  shipping accurate and successful AI features or products,
  such as chatbots, summarization, drafting, or document
  editing/comparison using LLMs, RAGs, and the right context
  for LLM calls.
- Analytics, Monitoring and Observability: Experience with
  making data driven decisions, based on product analytics
  and have experience using monitoring and observability
  tools like Datadog or equivalent to diagnose performance
  issues and ensure system health.
- Agile and Impact-Driven: Highly agile mindset with proven
  ability to work effectively in 0-to-1 setups, frequently
  make pivots, and translate ambiguity into concrete,
  impactful engineering work.
- Collaboration and Communication: Exceptional skills as a
  great collaborator with product, design, and QE teams,
  acting as a clear technical voice for customer-centric
  solutions.

Nice to Have Skills:

- AI Evaluation Experience: Experience building automated
  evaluation pipelines and defining technical metrics (e.g.,
  faithfulness, groundedness) to measure the quality of
  various AI features.
- Cloud Administration (GCP/Azure/AWS): Hands-on experience
  with managing infrastructure or administrative tasks on
  major cloud providers like GCP, Azure, or AWS.
- Legal Tech Domain: Prior experience in Legal Tech or other
  high-stakes domains (FinTech, HealthTech) where data
  accuracy and auditability are paramount.
- Document Processing: Experience working with PDF and DOCX
  parsing for document ingestion
- Familiarity with CI/CD systems (e.g., GitHub Actions,
  CircleCI)

Base Salary Ranges:

- Staff: $210,000 - $260,000
- Senior Staff: $240,000 - $295,000

The base salary range represents the minimum and maximum of
the salary range for this position based at our San
Francisco headquarters. The actual base salary offered for
this position will depend on numerous factors, including
individual proficiency, anticipated performance, and the
location of the selected candidate. Our base salary is just
one component of Ironclad’s competitive total rewards
package, which also includes equity awards (a new hire
grant, along with opportunities for additional awards
throughout your tenure), competitive health and wellness
benefits, and a commitment to career growth and development.

US Full-Time Employee Benefits at Ironclad:

- 100% health coverage for employees (medical, dental, and
  vision), and 75% coverage for dependents with buy-up plan
  options available
- Market-leading leave policies, including gender-neutral
  parental leave and compassionate leave
- Family forming support through Maven for you and your
  partner
- Paid time off - take the time you need, when you need it
- Monthly stipends for wellbeing, hybrid work, and (if
  applicable) cell phone use
- Mental health support through Modern Health, including
  therapy, coaching, and digital tools
- Pre-tax commuter benefits (US Employees)
- 401(k) plan with Fidelity with employer match (US Employees)
- Regular team events to connect, recharge, and have fun
- And most importantly: the opportunity to help build the
  company you want to work at

**UK Employee-specific benefits are included on our UK job
postings

Pursuant to the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, we will
consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and
conviction records.
responsibilities: Lead full-stack development of AI-powered features using TypeScript, Node.js, React, and PostgreSQL
Build and deploy production AI features including summarization, drafting, editing, and document comparison using LLMs
Architect and maintain highly scalable AI products for high-traffic SaaS environment
Establish prompt strategy and design optimal context input for LLM applications using RAG services
Collaborate with Product Managers, UX Designers, and QE teams to prototype and iterate on AI features
Work with platform teams to manage LLM APIs, ensuring resilient connections and efficient token usage
Advocate for customers by prioritizing user experience in technical decisions
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building and deploying LLM-
powered features using RAG, prompt engineering, and LLM APIs
to create production AI capabilities like summarization,
drafting, and document comparison. The position requires
hands-on work implementing AI/LLM applications, not just
supporting infrastructure.
